topic.what: Evaluating LLM cybersecurity certification knowledge across IT-OT specificity gradient using MCQA benchmarks
topic.how: Developed CyberCertBench suite from professional cert exams; Proposer-Verifier framework for interpretable difficulty explanations
topic.why_matters: Addresses LLM reliability risks in professional cybersecurity workflows; quantifies knowledge gaps in specialized OT domains

characteristics.evidence: Gemini 2.5 Pro 0506 achieves 98% on CyberMetric80; Claude 3.5 Sonnet 95%; Kimi K2 99%
characteristics.context: Top models saturate on academic benchmarks; specialized domains show degradation

characteristics.evidence: Only Claude 3.5 Sonnet achieves passing score; most models well below
characteristics.context: Formal standards knowledge is a measurable frontier for LLMs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fortinet NSE benchmark accuracy (vendor-specific procedural knowledge)
characteristics.evidence: No model approaches passing grade; highest score 62.4%
characteristics.context: Vendor-specific operational knowledge remains a significant frontier

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fortinet ICS/SCADA benchmark accuracy (conceptual OT security)
characteristics.evidence: Many top models achieve passing grade on conceptual OT knowledge
characteristics.context: General OT concepts are more accessible than specialized knowledge

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance relative to model size across open-weight models
characteristics.evidence: Qwen3 14B and Mistral Small 3.1 24B achieve comparable performance to Llama 3.1 405B
characteristics.context: Improvements in training methodology and data curation drive efficiency gains

characteristics.value: Clear positive trend with diminishing returns for largest models
characteristics.evidence: Figure 2 shows upward trend; smaller models show larger relative gains
characteristics.context: Progress driven by training methodology, not just parameter scaling
Experimental setup details provided but exact timing not specified

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1-score for Verifier difficulty classification using synthesized descriptions
characteristics.evidence: Multi-run synthesis yields most robust descriptions; baseline questions-only 36.7%
characteristics.context: Framework captures difficulty signals but benchmark saturation limits differentiation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of questions filtered as easy in PRO benchmark creation
characteristics.evidence: Fortinet ICS/SCADA removed 67.4%; Fortinet NSE removed 39.0%
characteristics.context: Question filtering based on 2-of-3 small model agreement

good_for: Comparing frontier models on IT/OT domains
Differentiating model capabilities via PRO benchmark
requires: API access or local model deployment

novelty: First comprehensive OT cybersecurity MCQA benchmark suite from professional certifications
Proposer-Verifier framework adapting IRT principles with LLM-generated interpretable descriptions
Quantified gradient of LLM knowledge specificity from IT to specialized OT domains
Data-driven PRO benchmark curation filtering easy questions via small model agreement
open_problems: Vendor-specific procedural knowledge gaps across all models
Formal standards (ISA/IEC 62443) knowledge deficiencies persist at large model scales
Self-referential limitation in Proposer-Verifier framework (Proposer shares evaluated models' knowledge gaps)
Selection bias in community-sourced certification questions despite expert curation
Need for more robust qualitative analysis reducing prompt sensitivity and variance
